articles.title: Inside the fairytale journey of Scotland captain Robertson
articles.summary: Andy Robertson has come a long way since playing amateur football. Now he is edging towards become Scotland's most capped men's player.

articles.title: Emotion and celebration for Deschamps' home farewell
articles.summary: There will be "emotion and celebration" in Lille on Monday as  manager Didier Deschamps prepares for his last match as France manager on home soil.

articles.title: Sinfield to visit 12 Super League grounds in final MND challenge
articles.summary: Kevin Sinfield will visit all 12 English Betfred Super League grounds in September during his seventh and final running challenge to raise money for motor neurone disease charities.

articles.title: Van Persie sacked by Feyenoord after 16 months
articles.summary: Feyenoord sack manager Robin van Persie after just one full season in charge of the Eredivisie club.

articles.title: Belief but work to do - Wales' two years under Bellamy
articles.summary: BBC Sport Wales looks at Craig Bellamy's time in charge of Wales as he reaches the midway stage of his four-year deal with the Football Association of Wales.

articles.title: O'Neill a 'no-brainer' for Celtic – now major rebuild awaits
articles.summary: With Martin O'Neill poised to be confirmed as Celtic manager after two interim spells last season, how big is the summer rebuild he is tasked with?

articles.title: Hodgkinson runs her fastest-ever 800m - but still finishes second
articles.summary: Keely Hodgkinson ran her fastest-ever 800m time on Sunday - but astonishingly still finished second at the Diamond League in Stockholm.
